mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in industrial production above trend signals a supply-driven expansion that boosts corporate earnings and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in supply-driven expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the unemployment rate below 5% signals a labor market tightening that boosts wage growth and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in labor market tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC rises above 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing starts above trend signals a demand-driven expansion that boosts consumer spending and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in demand-driven expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC falls below 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the federal funds rate above 3% signals a monetary tightening cycle that compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in monetary tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC rises above 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production below trend signals a supply-driven contraction that boosts corporate earnings and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in supply-driven contraction periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the unemployment rate above 7% signals a labor market loosening that boosts wage growth and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in labor market loosening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC rises above 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in housing starts below trend signals a demand-driven contraction that boosts consumer spending and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in demand-driven contraction periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC falls below 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window.
